title: Congress hangs RSS uniform at Bhopal election office over Natarajan's Rajya Sabha rejection
content: Congress workers protested the rejection of Rajya Sabha candidate Meenakshi Natarajan's nomination by hanging an RSS uniform on the locked gate of the Chief Electoral Officer's office in Bhopal.

title: 'Slap Stokes with a suspension': Ex-England legend on nightclub controversy
content: England cricket captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson face scrutiny after a reported team curfew breach and altercation. The incident has delayed squad announcements and intensified focus on Stokes' leadership. Former captain Geoffrey Boycott calls for a suspension, not a fine, emphasizing discipline. An investigation is ongoing, with the outcome pending.

title: Bill Gates warns Microsoft, Amazon, Google on data center push
content: Bill Gates has told the AI industry it does not have permission to drive up household electricity bills, warning Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft that the old utility-funded grid model is finished. Speaking on CNBC, the Microsoft co-founder said hyperscalers must now pick data centre sites where the economics and politics hold. With 48 projects worth $156 billion already blocked in 2025 and public opposition at record highs, the buildout is colliding with sentiment.

title: ITR filing: What documents are required to file your income tax return? Quick checklist
content: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Think of your documentation as evidence; the income tax department already has digital footprints of your transactions, and your job is to match them accurately.

title: Oracle founder Larry Ellison loses more than $47 billion in less than 7 days
content: Larry Ellison has seen his net worth plummet by over $47 billion in under a week, dropping from second to fifth richest globally. This significant loss is attributed to a broader selloff in technology and AI stocks, with Oracle shares experiencing a substantial decline ahead of its earnings report.

title: After Rs 370 biryani row, Gurugram company fires Himanshu Jangra
content: A web developer's joke about a ₹370 biryani date at a comedy show led to his job loss after viral backlash. His employer fired him, citing company values and workplace impact, but also sparked debate on accountability versus second chances for the 22-year-old.

title: Paying for the view? Check why the same flat costs more on a higher floor
content: Ever wondered why higher floor flats cost more? It's a mix of factors. Builders face increased construction costs for taller structures and stricter safety measures. Plus, buyers desire the elevated lifestyle: better views, more light, and privacy. Developers leverage this demand, often adding government charges to the final price, making those top-floor dream homes pricier.

title: US embassy creates $750 'fast pass' for business and tourist visa interviews
content: Starting July 1, 2026, the US State Department will pilot a new service allowing B1/B2 visa applicants to pay $750 for an expedited interview appointment. This temporary program aims to assess demand for expedited services and reduce strain on consular resources. While offering faster scheduling, it does not guarantee visa issuance or expedite administrative processing.

title: Buried in debt, Delhi man kills entire family, but unable to end his life; cops file chargesheet
content: A distinctive orange shirt became the key to apprehending Munchun Kewat in Rajasthan, accused of brutally murdering his wife and three daughters in Delhi. Financial distress from online gambling, leading to significant debt, is cited as the motive. Kewat reportedly confessed to the killings, believing his family would suffer otherwise.

title: Father-son duo gunned down in broad daylight in UP, shooter dies hours later; CCTV video surfaces
content: A long-standing feud turned deadly in Baghpat's Baraut town as a tent trader and his son were shot dead by gangster Varun Lohari, who also sustained fatal injuries. The incident triggered protests and market shutdowns. Police are investigating the years-old dispute, with 10 teams formed to apprehend Lohari's associates.

title: 'I don't want to be in two boats': Sushmita Dev quits TMC after Rajya Sabha resignation
content: Trinamool Congress faces another blow as MP Sushmita Dev resigns from Rajya Sabha, fueling speculation of a BJP switch after meeting Assam CM. This follows Sukhendu Sekhar Ray's departure, who made scathing remarks about the party. The TMC is navigating a challenging period marked by electoral losses and internal dissent.

title: Hyderabad realtor dumps ailing wife, marries her sister, then guns her down suspecting infidelity
content: A real estate businessman, previously arrested for possessing an illegal firearm intended to kill his second wife, has now allegedly shot and killed her at his Hyderabad home. The couple had marital disputes, leading the victim to live separately. The accused, who had spent time in jail for the earlier offense, lured her to his house before the fatal shooting.

title: Battling medical store's eye-drop blunder, depression: How Shubhi became India Girls' No. 1
content: Shubhi Gupta’s rise to World No. 4 in girls’ chess is a story of many things. After overcoming setbacks, including a vision problem that ruined her first independent foreign tournament and later competitive disappointments, she won national and world titles, excelled academically, and achieved major international norms. Now India’s top-ranked girl player, she is pursuing the most difficult titles.

title: Italy's Meloni congratulates PM Modi on surpassing Nehru's record, recalls recent Rome meeting
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni extended congratulations to Narendra Modi for becoming India's longest-serving elected Prime Minister, surpassing Jawaharlal Nehru's record. Meloni highlighted their recent meeting in Rome and the launch of a Special Strategic Partnership. She also recalled Modi's thoughtful gift of Melody toffees, a nod to their popular "Melodi" nickname.

title: Yamal, Endrick, Guler & Co: The young guns ready to storm FIFA World Cup 2026
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will have 48 nations, 104 matches and a thousand storylines, but nothing like the arrival of a new generation of players announcing themselves on the world's biggest stage. These are the players who watched the 2022 Qatar finals as schoolboys and are now walking into dressing rooms alongside Messi, Ronaldo and Mbappé. The expanded format means more room for young talent to showcase their game. Here are 10 under-21 players stepping onto the World Cup stage for the very first time.

title: Ahmedabad rental market: A look at affordability, connectivity and smart city benefits
content: Ahmedabad emerges as a top affordable rental market, boasting moderate rent growth and strong tenant appeal due to its blend of affordability, lifestyle, and connectivity. With a preference for 2 BHKs and semi-furnished homes, renters find value in its developing infrastructure, offering a comfortable urban experience without the high costs of major metros.

title: Fear of Recurrence: How immune memory helps cancer survivors face their worst nightmare
content: As we observe National Cancer Survivors Month, we delve into an exciting new era in the battle against cancer recurrence: the remarkable memory of our immune systems. Research indicates that our bodies can recognize and target cancer cells long after traditional treatment has concluded.

title: More than butter chicken: Why India can't stop craving Punjabi food
content: Punjabi cuisine, a cornerstone of Indian comfort food, has endured global culinary shifts by embodying emotion, hospitality, and home. From ancient roots to post-Partition dhabas and modern restaurants, its rich flavors and hearty simplicity continue to resonate, offering a taste of tradition and belonging that nourishes the soul.

title: 'Playing such a shot is mind-boggling': Ashwin tears into Pant
content: Rishabh Pant is a celebrated match-winner in Indian Test cricket, known for his aggressive style. However, his tendency to play high-risk shots, particularly in the 80s and 90s, has led to frustrating dismissals. Ravichandran Ashwin believes Pant, with over 50 Tests, needs to prioritize game awareness and convert promising scores into centuries for greater team rewards.

title: No Babar Azam, Shaheen Afridi as Pakistan name squad for Asiad 2026
content: Pakistan has named Sahibzada Farhan as captain for the 2026 Asian Games cricket tournament, leading a youthful, experimental squad. Several uncapped players feature, with Abdul Samad as vice-captain. This selection marks Farhan's first white-ball captaincy. Notably, established stars like Babar Azam and Shaheen Afridi are absent from the 15-member team.

title: Karnataka high court orders Rs 1 lakh fine on police official for kicking a lawyer
content: The High Court has sent a strong message regarding transparency in law enforcement by rejecting a Karnataka Sub-Inspector's request to dismiss an FIR over her purported assault on a lawyer. Not only was her plea dismissed, but she was also slapped with a Rs 1 lakh fine for withholding a previous court order that mandated the filing of the FIR.

title: Russian crude here to stay? Why India-US energy ties may be more about LPG, LNG than oil
content: Geopolitical developments in the last few months have dictated India’s energy supplies. Be it crude oil, LPG or LNG, India is largely dependent on the world to meet its needs, and the US-Iran war has disrupted supplies via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Hardik Pandya to miss Afghanistan ODIs due to low-grade quadricep sprain
content: Hardik Pandya will miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to a mild leg sprain sustained during training. The all-rounder, who was at peak fitness, will remain in Bengaluru for at least two weeks for recovery under the guidance of medical staff. This setback prevents him from joining the squad as planned.

title: Pak launches manhunt for four PoK protesters after crackdown killing 19 kids, 7 pregnant women
content: Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) administration launched a manhunt for protesters and offered a bounty for JAAC leaders after deadly clashes. An intelligence dossier details systematic violence by Pakistani forces, including targeting the JAAC and using live ammunition, resulting in numerous civilian deaths. India condemned the excessive force and urged international accountability for Pakistan's actions.

title: Quote of the day by Angelina Jolie
content: Angelina Jolie, reflecting on her role as Maria Callas, revealed that family, not work, defines her life, providing an antidote to loneliness. Her children's actions, like legally changing their surnames and expressing deep love, underscore this profound bond. This maternal strength has been a constant during her public legal battles, proving her children are her ultimate source of resilience.

title: Global leaders hail Modi as he surpasses Nehru's record of longest-serving elected PM
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has set a new record as India's longest-serving elected Prime Minister. He has now served more consecutive days in office than Jawaharlal Nehru. Leaders from the United States, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka have sent congratulations. They praised his leadership and public service. The US ambassador highlighted his decades of dedication.

title: TN CM Vijay mourns Bharathiraja; film fraternity hails a true legend
content: Tamil cinema mourns the passing of legendary filmmaker Bharathiraja. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin paid his respects, joining industry stalwarts who lauded his groundbreaking work. Bharathiraja, celebrated for his realistic portrayal of rural Tamil Nadu, leaves behind a rich cinematic legacy that inspired generations of filmmakers and actors.

title: 'Told him his son was in Jaipur': Man dies in ICU unaware Delhi hotel fire killed 8 family members
content: A 76-year-old man, Radheshyam Aggarwal, has passed away after losing eight relatives in a devastating Hauz Rani fire. His family had stayed at the B&B to be near him in the hospital, unaware of the tragedy. He died without knowing his wife, son, daughter-in-law, and granddaughters perished.

title: 'Need urgent help': Praggnanandhaa's teammate hit by visa issue ahead of World C'ships
content: Indian chess prodigy Pranesh Munirethinam faces uncertainty over his participation in the FIDE World Team Rapid and Blitz Championships in Hong Kong due to a pending visa. Coach RB Ramesh has sought urgent help to expedite the process. Pranesh is part of the ChessGurukul team led by R Praggnanandhaa and Vaishali Rameshbabu.

title: Bharathiraja: Meet the filmmaker who changed Tamil cinema
content: Renowned filmmaker Bharathiraja, celebrated for his realistic portrayal of rural life and human emotions, has passed away at 84. His groundbreaking film '16 Vayathinile' launched stars and redefined Tamil cinema. Bharathiraja's impactful career, spanning nearly five decades, earned him national awards and a Padma Shri, leaving an indelible legacy.

title: Watch: Messi silences injury concerns with stunning penalty in Argentina's 3-0 win
content: Lionel Messi eased fitness concerns ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 by scoring shortly after coming off the bench in Argentina's 3-0 win over Iceland. Returning from a hamstring injury, the captain converted a penalty just two minutes after entering the match. The victory boosted confidence as Argentina prepare to defend their World Cup title, with Messi set for his sixth tournament.

title: TNEA random number 2026 out today: Link and steps to check here
content: Tamil Nadu's Directorate of Technical Education has released the TNEA Random Number 2026 today, June 10. This unique 10-digit number, generated by the system, serves as a crucial tie-breaker for engineering admissions when candidates have identical marks. Applicants can retrieve their random number via the official TNEA admission portal login

title: Wife clings to hubby’s car after spotting him with another woman in UP; dragged 100 metres
content: A woman was dragged nearly 100 metres on the bonnet of a car in Bareilly after confronting her husband, whom she accused of infidelity. Bystanders intervened to rescue her after she sustained injuries and alleged assault and theft by the other woman. An FIR has been registered against both individuals.

title: Inside India's ODI dressing room: Why Gambhir, Gill must act before 2027 WC
content: India's ODI team begins World Cup preparations amid dressing room dynamics concerns. Shubman Gill's captaincy has seen mixed results, with senior players Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli facing questions about their future. Head coach Gautam Gambhir needs to assert authority in the ODI format to align the team for the 2027 tournament.

title: Bharathiraja passes away | Tributes pour in, TN govt to accord state honours
content: A towering personality in the film industry, Bharathiraja was fondly known as Iyakkunar Imayam (Pinnacle among Directors)

title: YouTuber Mukhtar arrested in two criminal cases, police informs Madras High Court
content: Justice C. Kumarappan told that he was arrested on June 9, 2026 by the Chennai cyber crime cell in connection with a complaint lodged by a woman functionary of BJP and also in connection with another case

title: Monsoon 2026 tracker LIVE: IMD issues orange alert in two districts in Kerala, thunderstorms, strong winds predicted
content: An orange alert has been issued for Alappuzha and Ernakulam districts of Kerala for three hours starting 10 a.m. on Wednesday (June 10, 2026) where isolated heavy rain is likely

title: In pictures: Director Bharathiraja, a transformative force of Tamil cinema
content: Widely considered one of the all-time greats of the industry, the legendary director was best known for his nuanced portrayal of life in rural Tamil Nadu

title: The early years when Bharathiraja and Ilaiyaraaja struggled to eke a living
content: Recalling the difficult days in a conversation with The Hindu in 2018, Ilaiyaraaja had said that in 1968, he, his brothers and a few friends boarded a bus to Chennai in search of a career in the film industry. The journey was anything but easy.

title: Sukhendu Sekhar Ray resigns from Rajya Sabha, and Trinamool Congress
content: The resignation brings to the fore differences among Trinamool MPs; speculation is rife that more MPs may follow suit

title: Another jolt to TMC, party MP Sushmita Dev resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: Sushmita Dev is the second MP of the TMC to resign this week, after Sukhendu Sekhar Ray had tendered his resignation from the party as well as the Rajya Sabha

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Congress delegation meets ECI over rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination
content: Congress leader Abhishek Singhvi said that all Meenakshi Natarajan had received was a court notice on a private complaint, asking why cognisance shouldn’t be taken

title: Director Bharathiraja (1941-2026)
content: Renowned Tamil film director Bharathiraja passed away in Chennai on Wednesday (June 10, 2026). He was 84.

title: Madhya Pradesh Rajya Sabha row: Democratic values being ‘killed under pressure’, says Amit Chavda
content: Reacting to the rejection of Congress Rajya Sabha candidate Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination papers in Madhya Pradesh, Amit Chavda said the decision was “not merely an administrative step” but “a killing of democratic values under political pressure”

title: Rajasthan HC steps in to save Udaipur’s water bodies from pollution, encroachments
content: The reports published in the Udaipur edition of Rajasthan Patrika, raised concerns regarding alleged encroachments on water bodies, unresolved disputes concerning lake boundaries and continuous construction activities around such areas

title: Hyderabad | Heavy rain caught us off guard, say Cyberabad Traffic Police
content: Commuters complain of two to four hours travel time to home. Officials say reassessment of catchment areas, drainage vents and associated infrastructure is required at locations that repeatedly witness waterlogging during the monsoon

title: Bond with PM Modi unaffected by Rajya Sabha nominations: H.D. Deve Gowda
content: The former PM’s statement comes after the Congress criticized BJP for denying him an opportunity to continue in the Rajya Sabha, calling it an “insult and humiliation”.

title: Free ride for women: A challenging balancing act awaits State, KSRTC
content: An estimate prepared by KSRTC reveals that the Corporation would incur a revenue loss of around ₹112 crore if the scheme were extended to all categories of buses, and about Rs 57 crore if it remained restricted to ordinary services in 90 days

title: Kerala Cabinet decides to implement free travel concession for women on KSRTC’s ordinary services from June 15
content: V.D. Satheesan says under the scheme titled ‘Priyadarshini’, concession will be available to women of all ages and irrespective of their financial background and transgenders. The implementation will add an additional annual financial burden of ₹750-₹800 crore for KSRTC

title: Trinamool rebellion reaches Delhi: 20 party MPs plan to form a separate bloc and support NDA
content: 20 of the 29 Lok Sabha MPs want to create a separate bloc and support the BJP-led NDA government in Parliament; Barasat Lok Sabha MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar is emerging as the leader of the group; West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari and Union Minister Bhupendra Yadav in contact with the rebel camp

title: INDIA bloc meeting: Opposition leaders seek unity, deliberate to redraw strategy
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge highlighted that the economic environment is extremely negative and new investments are not coming in at the pace required to generate new jobs

title: Mamata and Sonia hold talks on strategy, discuss alliance for future polls
content: The two leaders had a closed door meeting at Ms. Gandhi’s residence in Delhi, a day after the INDIA bloc meeting

title: Bharathiraja, the master filmmaker: How the ‘Iyakkunar Imayam’ captured the soul of Tamil Nadu for posterity
content: From ‘16 Vayathinile’ to ‘Vedham Pudhithu,’ ‘Iyakkunar Imayam’ Bharathiraja, who passed away today, made some of the most important Tamil films of all time

title: Bharathiraja’s masterpiece movie ‘Mudhal Mariyathai’: a Russian connection and initial misgivings
content: When many people had given up on Mudhal Mariyathai, Bharathiraja organised a special screening exclusively for women, recalled R. Selvaraj, Tamil film story writer and a close associate of director Bharathiraja

title: Kerala HC stays FIR against US citizens for flying drones in defence zone
content: Single Bench notes the offences charged against the petitioners were from a repealed law, namely, the Aircraft Act, 1934. The Act was replaced with Bharatiya Vayuyan Adhiniyam, 2024

title: Gaur that roamed 300 km across districts relocated to forest habitat
content: The guar from Tiruchi Forest Division moved through Pudukottai and Tiruvarur districts before reaching the coastal area of Muthupettai, from where it travelled to Mandapam through Mimisal, Thondi, and Ramanathapuram

title: Left parties block NH in Vijayawada over fuel, gas price hikes
content: Protesters accuse the Central and State governments of burdening the public through excessive taxation on petroleum products

title: Visakhapatnam steel blast claims a ninth life: a father’s last video tells his sons how to live without him
content: Paidiraju, 50, a contract worker with more than 90% burns, dies in hospital; the message he recorded for his sons on the way to treatment spreads online

title: Meenakshi Natarajan named as respondent in private complaint
content: A private complaint was filed in the Court of the 4th Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate in Hyderabad in 2025

title: ‘Guess the climax if you can’: The day Bharathiraja challenged film critics
content: In a 2008’s press screening of Bommalattam in 2008, Bharathiraja threw a challenge to journalists: predict the climax if you can

title: Chola and Hoysala idols at risk during Egmore Museum renovation
content: Ancient sculptures and temple pillars seized and repatriated by the Tamil Nadu Idol Wing lie lye amid construction equipment during ongoing renovation work at Chennai’s Egmore Government Museum, prompting concerns

title: Punjab on path to becoming startup hub of India, says CM Mann; distributes seed grants worth ₹1.07 crore
content: Under the Punjab Startup & Industrial Policy 2026, seed grant assistance has been enhanced from ₹3 lakh to ₹5 lakh

title: Former Maharashtra Minister files plea in Supreme Court against CBSE's three-language rule for Class 9 students
content: The plea filed by Fauzia Khan, an educationist and former Maharashtra Minister, contends that the CBSE's May 15 circular is arbitrary and unreasonable

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Congress candidate Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination rejected amid chaos in Madhya Pradesh
content: The rejection is based on allegations that Ms. Natarajan had “hidden” details of a case lodged against her in Telangana’s Hyderabad

title: Massive fire erupts at govt. building in Kolkata
content: The building serves as the South 24 Parganas district administrative office; although the blaze was largely contained, pockets of fire remained, the officials said

title: Army chief Upendra Dwivedi reviews operational preparedness at Northern Command in Jammu and Kashmir
content: The visit forms part of the Army’s ongoing focus on transformation, networking and data-centric warfare capabilities to strengthen operational effectiveness and preparedness

title: Telangana Chief Minister flags off 553 buses for SHGs
content: Revanth Reddy flags off 553 buses for self help groups, says women had excelled in public life as ministers, mayors, municipal chairpersons and corporation heads, and would play a greater role in governance with the implementation of 33% reservation for women

title: Ahmedabad Air India plane crash tragedy to teamwork: Gujarat's emergency response sets model of coordination
content: On June 12 last year, the London-bound Air India flight AI-171 crashed into the hostel complex of BJ Medical College in the Meghaninagar area moments after taking off

title: SIR in Karnataka: 96.27% voter mapping completed in Yadgir district
content: The 2002 electoral rolls have been considered as the benchmark, and compared with the existing 2025 electoral rolls through a mapping process

title: Bombay High Court restores pilot’s licence revoked 15 years ago, says DGCA’s suspension order ‘illegal’
content: The Bench said the DGCA’s licensing authority neither issued a show-cause notice to Jeetendra Krishna Varma nor granted him a hearing in person before taking the decision to suspend his licence

title: Anthropic India says it is different from U.S. entity
content: This was part of the hearing in the trademark violation case filed by Belagavi-based IT start up Anthropic Softwares Private Limited (ASPL)

title: 943 players take part in FIDE chess tournament at VIT-AP University
content: The Amaravati FIDE Rating Chess Tournament was organised by VIT-AP University and Andhra Chess Association

title: Thrissur seeks to revive fireworks manufacturing cluster, balancing modern safety with traditional practices amid festival regulations
content: Reviving the long-pending Thrissur fireworks cluster proposal gains momentum as stakeholders debate safer, modernised festival displays versus preserving traditional practices amid repeated calls for regulated local production

title: Experts question Kerala’s move to assess carrying capacity of forests and conduct wildlife census to mitigate conflicts
content: Environmentalists and wildlife experts argue that it risks reducing a complex ecological issue to wildlife numbers while overlooking habitat degradation, forest fragmentation and human pressures on forest ecosystems

title: Kerala rains: IMD issues orange alert in 2 districts; predicts thunderstorms, strong winds across State
content: On Tuesday, heavy rains accompanied by strong winds lashed several parts of Kerala, particularly in northern districts, leading to widespread waterlogging and prompting precautionary evacuation measures in some areas

title: JSW Infra secures Kolkata port container terminal bidding
content: The company has won a 30-year concession to develop container facilities at Netaji Subhas Dock, taking its planned capacity at the Kolkata Dock System to 1.4 million TEUs

title: UP CM launches ‘Project GANGA’
content: “Project Ganga will emerge as Uttar Pradesh’s new ‘Digital Expressway’, providing a new foundation for digital education, telemedicine, and smart agriculture.”

title: Mumbai BMC Opposition demands independent inquiry in ₹248 crore Mrinaltai Gore flyover project
content: The Leader of Opposition in BMC, Kishori Pednekar, along with the corporators, visited the flyover, raising concerns around the quality and cost of the road, demanding an independent inquiry

title: En Iniya Thamizh makkale, the master director is no more
content: Bharathiraja  inspired his assistant directors to carve their own path, and K. Bhagyaraj, R. Parthiban and Pandiarajan all evolved from the Bharathiraja school of thought

title: Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: "Long Story, Won't Comment On Mamata Didi," Says Sushmita Dev On Quitting Trinamool
content: West Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Crisis within the party escalated dramatically with a full-blown rebellion in the legislature and Parliament wings.

title: 12 Killed, 9 Injured In Mass Shooting In South Africa's Johannesburg
content: Police said the suspects arrived in a white Toyota Quantum, entered the settlement from two access points and opened fire at multiple locations before fleeing in the same vehicle.

title: Google Cloud Outage In India As 3rd-Party Data Centre Fire Triggers Shutdown
content: The cloud-computing unit said the fire led to an emergency power shutdown at the facility, isolating a local point of presence in Delhi.

title: Meet Indian-Origin Man Behind Drone Boat That Rescued US Pilots From Hormuz
content: Vibhav Altekar is a highly accomplished perception engineer with expertise in the field of autonomous systems and maritime technology.

title: World Witnessed Second-Hottest May On Record: EU's Climate Monitor
content: Records were broken in Britain, France, Ireland and Portugal last month as a "heat dome" of warm air from northern Africa pushed temperatures well above normal levels across western Europe.

title: Rs 11 Lakh Crore: How India's Electronics Sector Is Weathering Middle East Storm
content: India's mobile phone production has surged to Rs 5.45 lakh crore, while smartphone exports have jumped to more than Rs 2 lakh crore.

title: "Epstein Was A Monster, Master Manipulator": What His Ex-Aide Told Congress
content: Groff described Epstein as a "master manipulator" who kept his criminal activities hidden from those working closely with him.

title: Delhi Cop Demanded Rs 3 Crore Bribe To Influence CBI's Fake Drugs Probe
content: During a meeting in Delhi, a senior government official allegedly assured the accused he could secure them the outcome they wanted in the CBI case.

title: Opinion: Opinion | America Has Never, Ever Looked This Humiliated: The Story Of A Superpower's Fall
content: America's position was not this weak until recently; in fact, it was riding high. Then it chose to make all the wrong choices, and turned into a 'superpower' no one takes seriously anymore.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 500 Points Despite Fresh Escalation In US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude edges higher from seven-week low to $92 as US strikes Iran.

title: Trump Family Made $500 Million In Crypto Deal Before Firm's Collapse Hit Investors
content: Less than a year after the deal was celebrated at the Nasdaq stock exchange by Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump, AI Financial Corp. saw its share price plunge by more than 90 percent.

title: On Camera, Mumbai's BEST Bus Rams 2, Second Such Accident This Week
content: The incident occured around 11:40 am on Monday near the Poisar depot when the electric bus (route number A-277/38) was travelling from the Gorai depot.

title: Palantir To Challenge London Mayor Sadiq Khan Over Scrapped 50 Million Euros Police Deal
content: Sadiq Khan's office says the procurement process failed to demonstrate value for money and did not follow required procedures

title: Indian-Origin Cop Killed In Canada After 18-Year-Old Man Hit Him With Car
content: The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) have arrested an 18-year-old man for the first-degree murder of Bali. The accused has been charged with two counts each of flight from police and dangerous driving.

title: Uranium Ban To Site Closures: What US Wants From Iran In Nuclear Talks
content: If Iran accepts this demand, along with the other three conditions being discussed, it would be a much stricter arrangement than the nuclear deal reached in 2015.

title: Airfares May Get Costlier As Jet Fuel Price Rises 10% For Domestic Airlines
content: Oil companies have introduced a voluntary price stabilisation scheme that allows domestic airlines to lock in fuel prices for up to three years.

title: Death Count In 7.8 Magnitude Philippines Earthquake Rises To 46
content: The 7.8-magnitude tremor just off the coast of Mindanao on Monday brought down buildings, triggered landslides and set off tsunami warnings across a swathe of the southern island.

title: "4,399 Days And Counting": PM Modi Becomes Longest-Serving Elected Prime Minister
content: PM Modi on Wednesday became the longest-serving prime minister, with an unbroken tenure of 4,399 days in office.

title: Maharashtra Cabinet approves expansion of administrative departments to 45
content: The newly formed departments will get separate arrangements for cash branches and registration branches. However, no new posts will be created during restructuring exercise

title: Delhi data centre fire may have destroyed equipment worth hundreds of crores
content: The fire, which broke out in the early morning hours of June 5, has led to major equipment losses for companies hosting servers there, and caused disruptions for many local internet operators.

title: One month on, 14 hostages freed in Manipur
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More

title: Congress’ Natarajan’s RS nomination rejected, BJP close to 3/3 in MP
content: EC cannot overturn a returning officer’s decision during scrutiny . the stakes had already been running high after BJP fielded Mahesh Kewat as a third candidate . BJP, with 164 MLAs in the 230-member assembly, is comfortably placed to bag two seats .

title: Kerala Univ asks prof to pay Rs 17L over SBI lapse
content: KU has asked the director of its centre for Latin American Studies to pay around Rs 17 lakh . the bank had deducted $20,000 from the centre's account at Thejaswini branch . he was carrying out instructions to pay Rs 20,000 to journalist Milan Sime Martinic .

title: Modi surpasses Nehru as longest-serving elected PM: Tracing the road to 4,399 days in 12 charts
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: NMC vacancies delaying medical admissions: Amicus flags lack of transparency
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More
